title: 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship for Women: WCSF Internship Funding in Washington, D.C.
summary: Students and early-career professionals interning on Capitol Hill often face significant financial challenges, especially due to the high cost of living in Washington, D.C. To address this barrier and promote inclusivity, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation (WCSF) is offering its Summer 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship —a funding opportunity designed to support women pursuing internships in the U.S. legislative environment. This scholarship not only provides financial assistance but also opens doors to valuable networking opportunities and mentorship from professionals serving on the WCSF Board. It is a strategic initiative aimed at increasing the representation and participation of women—especially those from diverse and underrepresented backgrounds—in the legislative workforce. About the Scholarship The WCSF Scholarship is dedicated to broadening the voices and experiences of women on Capitol Hill . Recognizing that internships often serve as a critical entry point into full-time roles in government and policy, the program seeks to strengthen the pipeline by supporting women at the very beginning of their careers. A key focus of the scholarship is to ensure that women from middle- and low-income backgrounds are not excluded from these opportunities due to financial constraints. By reducing the burden of living and working in Washington, D.C., WCSF enables more women to contribute their perspectives and expertise to the legislative process. Scholarship Benefits Recipients of the WCSF Scholarship will gain: Financial support to help cover expenses during their Capitol Hill internship Professional networking opportunities with leaders and policymakers Career mentorship and guidance from members of the WCSF Board Exposure to the inner workings of the U.S. legislative system Application Deadline and Process Application Deadline: April 15, 2026 (11:59 PM EST) Interview Notification: Finalists will be contacted to schedule interviews with the selection committee Final Award Announcement: No later than May 24, 2026 Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , meaning early submission is highly encouraged to increase your chances of selection. Why This Scholarship Matters Internships on Capitol Hill are often a stepping stone to influential careers in government, policy, and public service. However, without financial support, many qualified candidates—particularly women from underrepresented backgrounds—may be unable to pursue these opportunities. The WCSF Scholarship plays a crucial role in leveling the playing field , ensuring that talent and passion—not financial limitations—determine who gets a seat at the table in shaping public policy. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for the scholarship, applicants must: Have applied for an internship with: A Member, Delegate, or Resident Commissioner of Congress, OR A U.S. Senate or House committee, caucus, or leadership office in Washington, D.C. Provide a confirmation letter of the internship before receiving funding Identify as a woman Be at least 18 years old Hold a diploma from an accredited high school Selection Criteria Applications will be evaluated based on several important factors: Demonstrated financial need , including expenses related to the internship Alignment between the internship and the applicant’s academic and career goals Applications for internships with significant responsibility and impact Overall quality of application materials , including resume, essays, and references Academic performance and track record Terms and Conditions Recipients of the scholarship are required to: Complete the full internship as described in their application Provide accurate and truthful information, particularly regarding financial need Failure to meet these requirements may result in forfeiture or repayment of scholarship funds . Exceptions will only be made in rare and exceptional circumstances, as determined by the WCSF Board of Directors. Additionally, the scholarship funds are non-transferable and can only be used for the internship specified in the application. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their applications before the deadline of April 15, 2026. Since applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, applying early is strongly recommended. APPLY HERE By supporting aspiring women leaders at the start of their careers, the Women’s Congressional Staff Foundation is helping to build a more inclusive, representative, and effective legislative workforce. Discover more similar opportunities here. The post 2026 Capitol Hill Scholarship for Women: WCSF Internship Funding in Washington, D.C. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: FAO Girls in ICT Day 2026 E-Mentorship on AI in Agrifood Systems
summary: In celebration of International Girls in ICT Day 2026 , the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) has announced an exciting global opportunity for young women to participate in a virtual e-mentorship program focused on artificial intelligence (AI) and its role in agrifood systems. This initiative is designed to bring together aspiring young women, experienced mentors, and global stakeholders to engage in meaningful discussions about the future of technology and its impact on food systems worldwide. About the Initiative The E-Mentorship program is part of FAO’s broader efforts to address the digital gender divide by equipping girls and young women with the knowledge, confidence, and networks needed to thrive in technology-driven fields. Through this global online event, participants will explore both the challenges and opportunities that AI presents in transforming agrifood systems. Agrifood systems are increasingly being shaped by digital innovations, and AI is playing a critical role in improving agricultural productivity, sustainability, and resilience. However, women and girls remain underrepresented in these technological advancements. This mentorship program seeks to bridge that gap by encouraging greater participation and ensuring that girls are not only beneficiaries of innovation but also active contributors to its development. Event Theme and Objectives The 2026 edition of the event will take place on April 23 under the theme: “AI for Development: Girls Shaping the Digital Future.” This theme underscores the importance of inclusive innovation and highlights the vital role that girls and young women play in accelerating the adoption and ethical use of AI technologies. It emphasizes that empowering girls in ICT is not only a matter of equality but also a necessity for sustainable global development. The key objectives of the event include: Promoting awareness of AI applications in agrifood systems Encouraging girls to pursue careers and opportunities in ICT and related fields Providing mentorship and role models from diverse professional backgrounds Facilitating knowledge exchange between participants and experts Strengthening global efforts to close the digital gender gap Structure of the E-Mentorship Session A central component of this initiative is a two-hour virtual mentorship session tailored specifically for young women interested in AI and agrifood systems. This interactive session will feature: Expert Mentors: Professionals from the private sector, academia, and United Nations agencies will participate as mentors. These individuals bring a wealth of experience in AI, agriculture, and digital innovation. Career Insights: Mentors will share their personal career journeys, including the challenges they faced, the milestones they achieved, and the lessons they learned along the way. Aspirational Guidance: Participants will gain valuable advice on how to navigate educational and career pathways in technology and agrifood sectors. Interactive Q A Session: Attendees will have the opportunity to ask questions, seek guidance, and engage directly with experts. The session is designed to be highly engaging and participatory, ensuring that attendees not only listen but actively contribute to discussions. Certification and Participation Benefits All participants who successfully attend and complete the mentorship session will receive a certificate of participation. This certificate serves as recognition of their engagement and commitment to learning about AI applications in agrifood systems. Beyond certification, participants will benefit from: Exposure to global perspectives on AI and agriculture Opportunities to network with professionals and peers Increased awareness of career pathways in emerging technological fields Enhanced confidence to participate in digital innovation spaces Eligibility Criteria FAO invites applications from young women around the world who meet the following requirements: Must be female and aged between 18 and 25 years Demonstrated interest in technology, agriculture, or the intersection of both Currently enrolled in or recently graduated from a post-secondary institution (university or vocational program) Academic background in STEM or agriculture is advantageous but not mandatory Strong motivation to learn about AI applications in agrifood systems Commitment to attend the full mentorship session and actively participate in all activities Working proficiency in English Willingness to provide feedback after the session to support the improvement of future programs Application Process and Deadline Interested candidates are encouraged to apply by April 5, 2026 . Early registration is recommended due to the global nature of the program and the potential for high demand. Click here to Register Applicants should ensure they meet all eligibility criteria and are available to attend the full session on April 23, 2026. Advancing Inclusion in the Digital Age This initiative reflects FAO’s commitment to fostering inclusive digital transformation in agrifood systems. By investing in the next generation of female leaders in ICT, the organization aims to create a more equitable and innovative future. Programs like this not only provide knowledge and mentorship but also inspire confidence and ambition among young women. As AI continues to reshape industries worldwide, ensuring that girls are equipped to participate meaningfully in this transformation is essential. The E-Mentorship for Girls in ICT Day 2026 represents a valuable opportunity for young women to gain insights, build networks, and take a step forward in shaping the digital future of agrifood systems. Discover more similar opportunities here The post Apply Now: FAO Girls in ICT Day 2026 E-Mentorship on AI in Agrifood Systems appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Unlock New Markets: Apply for the AfCFTA Agribusiness SME Matchmaking Programme 2026
summary: African agribusinesses are at the heart of the continent’s economic transformation. If you are an export-ready Small or Medium Enterprise (SME) operating within the agri-food value chain, this is your opportunity to scale beyond borders and tap into regional and international markets through the African Continental Free Trade Area (AfCFTA). The AfCFTA Agribusiness SME Matchmaking Programme 2026 is designed to help ambitious businesses expand their reach, strengthen their export capabilities, and build meaningful trade partnerships across Africa and with global markets like Switzerland. What Is the Programme About? This initiative is a targeted business support programme aimed at empowering agribusiness SMEs to leverage AfCFTA opportunities. A total of 30 high-potential businesses will be selected through a competitive process to receive tailored support that enhances their readiness for cross-border trade. Key Benefits of the Programme Selected participants will gain access to a comprehensive support package, including: Specialized Training on AfCFTA Opportunities Understand how to navigate and benefit from Africa’s largest free trade agreement. Market Expansion Support Position your business to enter new regional and international markets. Packaging and Labelling Assistance Improve your product presentation to meet international standards. Business Matchmaking Buyer Connections Engage directly with potential buyers through curated B2B sessions. Access to Trade Fairs and Market Linkage Events Showcase your products and connect with key industry players. Tailored Coaching by Sales Experts Develop effective go-to-market strategies with expert guidance. Participation in AfCFTA Flagship Events Gain visibility and network at high-level continental trade events. Who Should Apply? This opportunity is ideal for agribusiness SMEs that: Operate within agriculture or food value chains Are export-ready or preparing to scale internationally Are committed to actively participating in programme activities Seek to explore new trade and partnership opportunities Women-led and youth-led enterprises are strongly encouraged to apply. Programme Impact By participating in this programme, SMEs will not only strengthen their competitiveness but also gain the tools and networks needed to thrive in the evolving African trade landscape. With AfCFTA unlocking a market of over 1.3 billion people, this initiative positions your business at the forefront of continental trade integration. Application Deadline All interested applicants must submit their Expression of Interest before: 6 April 2026 Late applications will not be considered, so early submission is highly recommended. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more grant opportunities, click here . Confidentiality Notice All submitted information will be handled with strict confidentiality and used solely for analytical and programme evaluation purposes. Final Thoughts This is more than just a training programme—it is a gateway to scaling your agribusiness across Africa and beyond. If you are ready to grow, connect, and compete on a larger stage, this opportunity could be your next big step. title: eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 in San Jose: High-Paying Opportunity for U.S. University Students
summary: The eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 is a highly competitive, full-time internship opportunity designed for undergraduate students in the United States who are eager to gain hands-on experience in product management within a global technology company. This program stands out for its strong emphasis on real-world product development, cross-functional collaboration, and the integration of AI-powered tools in modern product workflows. With a structured 6-month duration and a generous hourly pay of up to $47, this co-op provides a rare opportunity to work directly on products used by millions of users worldwide while building critical skills in product strategy, execution, and analytics. About the eBay Product Management Co-op This co-op program is tailored for emerging talent, especially Gen Z students, who are interested in building digital products for a global audience. Participants are embedded within product teams and collaborate closely with engineering, design, research, and data professionals. The program emphasizes: AI-enabled product discovery and decision-making Real-world product lifecycle exposure Ownership of meaningful product outcomes Mentorship from experienced product leaders By the end of the program, participants are expected to deliver a comprehensive presentation showcasing their contributions, learnings, and strategic recommendations. Key Responsibilities During the co-op, selected candidates will: Product Discovery and Research Conduct user research and analyze customer insights Perform competitive analysis Use data to identify opportunities and define problems Product Development and Execution Define product requirements and user stories Collaborate with engineering and design teams Participate in Agile or Scrum-based workflows AI and Innovation Use AI-powered tools for prototyping, research, and iteration Accelerate workflows using automation and analytics tools Performance Tracking and Analysis Monitor product performance metrics Evaluate post-launch results Generate insights for continuous improvement Communication and Collaboration Work cross-functionally with multiple teams Present findings and updates to stakeholders Align product goals across departments Eligibility Criteria Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to: Undergraduate students currently enrolled full-time in a U.S.-accredited university Students graduating between December 2026 and Summer 2027 Eligible Fields of Study Applicants should be pursuing degrees in: Product Management Business Administration Entrepreneurship Computer Science Engineering Economics Design or Industrial Design Related disciplines Who Should Apply This co-op is ideal for: Students passionate about product management and technology Individuals interested in building user-centric digital experiences Candidates looking to gain experience in a major global tech company Those who want to work at the intersection of AI, data, and product innovation Apply here Countries Eligible Only students enrolled in accredited universities within the United States Must have legal authorization to work in the U.S. This opportunity is not open to international applicants without U.S. work authorization. Work Authorization Requirements (Very Important) This role has strict eligibility conditions: Applicants must have U.S. work authorization Visa sponsorship is NOT provided Students on F1, CPT, OPT, or requiring H1B sponsorship are NOT eligible Required Qualifications Applicants should demonstrate: Experience (academic, internship, or project-based) in product management, technology, analytics, or consulting Familiarity with AI tools such as generative AI, data analysis platforms, or automation tools Strong analytical thinking and data-driven decision-making skills Excellent communication skills (written and verbal) Ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment A growth mindset with curiosity and adaptability Preferred Qualifications While not mandatory, the following will strengthen your application: Experience with Agile or Scrum methodologies Understanding of digital marketplaces or platform-based businesses Basic technical knowledge (APIs, backend systems, architectures) Experience solving ambiguous or complex problems Cross-functional collaboration experience Benefits of the Program Financial Benefits Competitive hourly pay: $42–$47 Full-time work experience over 6 months Career Development Exposure to real-world product management Mentorship from industry professionals Hands-on experience with AI-driven product tools Professional Impact Opportunity to contribute to products used globally Build a strong portfolio project Gain experience highly valued by top tech companies Location and Work Setup Primary Location: San Jose, California Work Mode: Hybrid (combination of in-office and remote work) Relocation: Candidates must be willing to relocate if not already in the area Important Dates Interview Day: April 10, 2026 (mandatory availability required) Interview Duration: 1.5 hours (virtual) Application Policy: Only one application per candidate is allowed Start Date: June 15, 2026 End Date: December 11, 2026 Applicants must be available for the scheduled interview day. No alternative dates or rescheduling options are provided, making this a strict and critical deadline. Deadline 8th April 2026 The eBay Product Management Co-op 2026 is a premier opportunity for students aiming to break into product management. With strong compensation, hands-on experience, and exposure to cutting-edge AI tools, this program offers both professional growth and real-world impact. title: FULLY FUNDED OML MENA FELLOWSHIP 2026: Leadership Opportunity for Young Changemakers in the Middle East and North Africa
summary: If you are a young leader passionate about creating real impact in your community, the One Million Leaders (OML) MENA Fellowship 2026 is one of the most transformative fully funded opportunities you can apply for right now. This prestigious leadership program is designed to equip the next generation of changemakers with practical skills, global networks, and hands-on experience to drive sustainable change across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region. What is the OML MENA Fellowship 2026? The OML MENA Fellowship is a highly impactful leadership development program designed to empower young leaders with skills in sustainability, social innovation, and ethical leadership. It is part of a global initiative aiming to train one million leaders by 2030. The program focuses on building leaders who are not just thinkers but active problem-solvers working on real-world challenges in their communities. Participants engage in structured training, mentorship, and project implementation phases that translate ideas into measurable impact. Why You Should Apply for the OML MENA Fellowship The OML Fellowship is more than just a training program—it is a life-changing leadership experience. Here is what makes it stand out: 1. Fully Funded Participation Selected fellows receive access to all program components at no cost, including training, mentorship, and resources. 2. World-Class Leadership Training Participants undergo intensive training based on five core pillars: Sustainability Essentials Ethical Leadership Social Innovation Personal Development Community Impact 3. Mentorship and Global Networking Fellows connect with: Global mentors Industry experts Like-minded young leaders across the MENA region and beyond 4. Real Impact Through Projects Participants design and implement community-based projects addressing real social or environmental challenges. Some projects may even receive micro-grant funding for execution. 5. Alumni and Long-Term Opportunities Graduates join a global alumni network that offers: Continued mentorship Collaboration opportunities Access to future funding and programs Program Structure: What to Expect The OML MENA Fellowship is structured into four major phases: Phase 1: Intensive Training Fellows undergo online training sessions focused on leadership and sustainability. Phase 2: Leadership Challenge Participants train and mentor a group of young leaders (“Champions”), multiplying their impact. Phase 3: Act-for-Change Project Fellows implement real-life projects that address key issues in their communities. Phase 4: Graduation Alumni Network Successful participants graduate and join a global leadership network. Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria) The OML MENA Fellowship is specifically designed for young changemakers in the MENA region. Eligible Categories of Applicants: Young leaders Social entrepreneurs Community organizers Sustainability advocates Students and early-career professionals Basic Requirements: Age: Typically 18 to 40 years Strong interest in social impact, sustainability, or community development Demonstrated leadership or involvement in a project or initiative Willingness to mentor and lead others Commitment to complete the program Access to internet for virtual sessions Eligible Countries and Regions The fellowship is open to applicants from the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region, including but not limited to: Egypt Morocco Tunisia Algeria Jordan Lebanon Saudi Arabia United Arab Emirates فلسطين (Palestine) Iraq Sudan Somalia Yemen Qatar Kuwait Applicants must either be citizens or residents of MENA countries. Key Benefits of the OML MENA Fellowship 2026 Participating in this fellowship gives you access to: Fully funded leadership training Mentorship from global experts Opportunity to lead and train other young leaders Access to micro-grants for impactful projects Exposure to international networks Skill development in sustainability and innovation Recognition as an OML Fellow Why This Fellowship Matters in 2026 In today’s world, leadership is no longer just about authority—it is about impact, innovation, and sustainability. The OML MENA Fellowship aligns with global development goals by nurturing leaders who can: Address climate challenges Drive social innovation Build inclusive communities Empower future generations This fellowship is particularly valuable for young people who want to move from ideas to action and become part of a global movement for change. Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide To apply for the OML MENA Fellowship 2026, follow these steps: Step 1: Online Registration Complete the official application form via the OML platform. Step 2: Submit Project Details Provide information about: Your leadership experience Your social impact work Your vision for change Step 3: Screening Interviews Shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews. Step 4: Final Selection Successful applicants are admitted into the fellowship program. Apply here Deadline 20th April 2026 (GMT+2) The OML MENA Fellowship 2026 is one of the most comprehensive and impactful fully funded leadership programs available for young people in the MENA region. It offers not just knowledge, but real-world experience, funding opportunities, and a powerful global network. title: WILD Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Leadership Fellowship for Women Driving Social Impact
summary: The WILD Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious global leadership fellowship designed to empower women across the world to lead transformational social impact initiatives. This opportunity stands out as one of the most impactful global fellowships focused on equipping women leaders with the tools, networks, and platforms needed to influence change at local, regional, and global levels. According to Official Fellowship Page, the program targets women at different career stages who are committed to advancing leadership, sustainability, and social change initiatives in their communities and beyond. What is the WILD Fellowship 2026? The WILD Fellowship is a high-impact global leadership program tailored specifically for women who are passionate about creating meaningful change. It brings together a diverse cohort of participants from across continents to engage in leadership forums, capacity-building sessions, and collaborative learning experiences. This fellowship is structured to provide both online and in-person engagement opportunities, ensuring accessibility for participants worldwide while still offering immersive leadership experiences. The program is unique because it does not only focus on academic or technical skills, but also emphasizes: Leadership transformation Personal growth and empowerment Global networking and collaboration Real-world impact through social initiatives Participants become part of a powerful global network of women leaders committed to addressing pressing global challenges. Fellowship Overview and Key Highlights The WILD Fellowship 2026 offers a comprehensive experience that goes beyond traditional training programs. It combines mentorship, peer learning, and leadership development in a dynamic and inclusive environment. Core Highlights of the Fellowship Global leadership development program for women Access to international forums and leadership events Opportunity to connect with influential women leaders worldwide Blended learning format (online and in-person sessions) Focus on social impact, sustainability, and community leadership The fellowship is particularly beneficial for individuals seeking to expand their leadership influence while working on projects that create real change. Who Can Apply for the WILD Fellowship 2026? The fellowship is open to a broad and diverse group of women leaders across the globe. It is intentionally inclusive, ensuring representation from different regions, sectors, and career stages. Eligible Categories of Applicants Early-career professionals Mid-career professionals Senior-level leaders Social entrepreneurs Community leaders and activists Professionals working in NGOs, government, or private sector Gender Requirement The fellowship is specifically designed for women committed to leadership and social impact. Eligible Countries and Regions The WILD Fellowship is a global opportunity, meaning applicants can apply from: Africa Asia Europe North America South America Oceania There are no strict geographical limitations, making it accessible to women from both developed and developing regions. Required Qualifications and Eligibility Criteria While the fellowship is inclusive, applicants must demonstrate strong leadership potential and a commitment to social change. Key Eligibility Requirements Applicants should: Demonstrate a clear commitment to social impact or community development Show evidence of leadership experience or potential Be actively engaged in initiatives that contribute to positive change Possess strong communication and collaboration skills Be willing to participate fully in fellowship activities (online and/or in-person) Unlike many academic programs, the WILD Fellowship places greater emphasis on impact and leadership rather than formal academic qualifications. Benefits of the WILD Fellowship 2026 The fellowship offers a wide range of benefits that extend beyond financial support. It is designed to create long-term value for participants. Professional and Leadership Benefits Advanced leadership training and development Exposure to global best practices in social impact Opportunities to lead and collaborate on international initiatives Networking Opportunities Access to a global network of women leaders Collaboration with changemakers from diverse sectors Lifelong connections with fellowship alumni Personal Growth Enhanced confidence and leadership identity Improved communication and strategic thinking skills Personal transformation through mentorship and peer engagement Why You Should Apply for the WILD Fellowship The WILD Fellowship is not just another leadership program—it is a transformational journey for women who want to make a difference. Here’s why this opportunity stands out: It focuses on real-world impact, not just theory It connects you with a global community of leaders It enhances both personal and professional growth It provides a platform to amplify your work and influence For women in Africa, Asia, and other underrepresented regions, this fellowship offers a powerful opportunity to gain global visibility and recognition. Application Process for the WILD Fellowship 2026 While specific steps may vary slightly, the general application process includes: Online Application Submission Complete the official application form Personal Statement or Essays Highlight leadership experience and social impact work Supporting Documents May include CV, references, or project details Selection Process Shortlisting and potential interviews Applicants are encouraged to present a compelling narrative that showcases their passion, leadership journey, and vision for impact. Apply here Deadline 31st March 2026 The WILD Fellowship 2026 is one of the most impactful global fellowships for women seeking to scale their leadership and social impact initiatives. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post WILD Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Leadership Fellowship for Women Driving Social Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ethereum Foundation PhD Fellowship 2026: $24,000 Grant for Cutting-Edge Research in Blockchain, AI, Economics & More
summary: The Ethereum ecosystem continues to push the boundaries of innovation—and now, the Ethereum Foundation is investing in the next generation of academic leaders. The Ethereum Foundation PhD Fellowship Program 2026 is a prestigious, fully funded opportunity designed to support groundbreaking academic research led by current PhD students. With $24,000 USD in funding over one year , this program aims to accelerate high-impact research that contributes to the evolution of decentralized systems and Ethereum-based technologies. About the Fellowship Program The 2026 fellowship is a fellowship-style grant initiative supporting interdisciplinary academic research aligned with Ethereum’s mission. The program is open to PhD students across a wide range of fields, encouraging innovative thinking at the intersection of blockchain and real-world systems. Selected fellows will: Receive $24,000 USD over one year (as a supplement to their stipend) Join a cohort of 9–10 fellows globally Contribute to open-access academic research Potentially qualify for extended funding based on performance Key Research Areas Applicants are encouraged to submit proposals aligned with Ethereum-related themes, including but not limited to: Economics: Mechanism design, contract theory, institutional economics Political Science: Governance, political economy, comparative systems Computer Science: AI, machine learning, distributed systems Business Finance: Organizational theory, MIS, operations research Public Policy Urban Planning: Infrastructure, governance, smart cities Sociology: Economic sociology, science technology studies Additionally, specific research tracks include: Decentralized economic models Smart contract applications for SMEs Programmable governance systems Financial infrastructure and compliance efficiency Urban coordination systems AI-powered protocol security On-chain capital formation Open-source sustainability models Agent-based smart contract systems Funding Fellowship Benefits $24,000 USD stipend (paid over one year) Opportunity for continuation funding Global recognition from the Ethereum ecosystem Contribution to real-world blockchain innovation Open-access publication exposure Eligibility Requirements To apply, candidates must: Be actively enrolled in a PhD program Submit a formal research proposal (in English, PDF format) Provide a public repository or past research work Commit to open-access publication Demonstrate ongoing or planned academic dissemination (journal/conference) Applicants may submit multiple proposals, provided each aligns with a specific research track. Selection Criteria Applications are reviewed holistically based on: Research impact and relevance Quality and clarity of proposal Applicant and supervisor’s academic background Long-term contribution to the Ethereum ecosystem Ability to translate research for non-technical audiences Application Timeline Applications Open: February 2, 2026 Deadline: April 1, 2026 (23:59 AoE) Results Notification: Within 6 weeks after the deadline How to Apply Interested candidates should prepare a detailed proposal aligned with one of the program’s research focus areas and submit it through the official Ethereum Foundation fellowship page. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities, click here . Why You Should Apply This fellowship is more than funding—it’s an opportunity to contribute to one of the most transformative technological ecosystems in the world. Whether your expertise lies in economics, AI, governance, or urban systems, this program offers a platform to turn your research into real-world impact. title: Oxford University CSAE Visiting Fellowship 2027: Fully Funded Research Residency for African Economists
summary: The CSAE Visiting Fellowship 2027 at the University of Oxford is now open for applications, offering a prestigious, fully funded opportunity for African economists to undertake a research residency at one of the world’s leading academic institutions. Hosted by the Centre for the Study of African Economies (CSAE) , this fellowship supports scholars in advancing their research, building global collaborations, and engaging with cutting-edge discussions on African economic development. The programme is designed to provide an intellectually stimulating environment where fellows can refine their research, interact with leading academics, and contribute to global policy and academic debates. About the CSAE Visiting Fellowship The CSAE Visiting Fellowship is a highly competitive academic programme that brings together outstanding African economists to Oxford for a focused research residency. Fellows spend time engaging with CSAE faculty, attending seminars, and participating in workshops while advancing their own independent research projects. The fellowship aims to strengthen academic networks, foster collaboration, and support high-quality research on African economies. Fellowship Duration and Location Duration: January to March 2027 Location: University of Oxford, United Kingdom During this period, fellows are fully immersed in the academic environment at Oxford, benefiting from access to world-class research facilities and intellectual resources. What the Fellowship Offers Selected fellows receive comprehensive financial and academic support, including: 1. Full Funding Package International flights to and from Oxford Accommodation throughout the fellowship period A stipend to cover living expenses 2. Academic and Research Opportunities Access to CSAE seminars, workshops, and conferences Opportunities to present and refine research One-on-one engagement with leading economists and faculty 3. Networking and Collaboration Interaction with global researchers and scholars Opportunities for long-term academic collaboration Exposure to frontier research topics in development economics Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the CSAE Visiting Fellowship, applicants must: Be a citizen of an African country Hold a PhD in Economics Be actively pursuing a career in economics or related research fields The programme is specifically targeted at scholars working on African economic issues and development research. Why You Should Apply The CSAE Visiting Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to: Conduct high-impact research at the University of Oxford Engage with leading experts in African economic development Receive valuable feedback on ongoing research Build global academic and professional networks Strengthen your profile as a researcher in economics Application Process Applicants are required to submit their application through the official platform, including all necessary documentation as specified in the application guidelines. The selection process is competitive and based on: Academic qualifications and research experience Relevance and quality of research work Potential for contribution to the CSAE academic community Application Timeline Application Deadline: May 15, 2026 (12:00 noon BST) Fellowship Period: January – March 2027 Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early and ensure all required documents are submitted before the deadline. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their applications through the official portal: Apply for the CSAE Visiting Fellowship 2027 by completing the Application Form. Click HERE to learn more about the CSAE Visiting Fellowship 2027. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE. title: Lens of Change: Paid Filmmaking Opportunity with AU–EU Youth Voices Lab (Apply Anytime)
summary: The AU–EU Youth Voices Lab – Power of the Collective is expanding its network of young filmmakers across Africa and Europe. This exciting opportunity invites creatives aged 18–35 to join a Young Filmmakers Database and contribute to documenting powerful stories of youth-led advocacy and social change. If you are passionate about storytelling and want to use film to amplify real impact, this is your chance to get funded while building an international portfolio. About the Young Filmmakers Database The AU–EU Youth Voices Lab is creating a database of talented filmmakers from selected countries across Africa and Europe. This database will serve as a pool of creatives who can be engaged on a project basis to document: Youth-led advocacy moments Community impact stories Social transformation initiatives Selected filmmakers will produce short documentaries (3–15 minutes) that highlight narratives of change, resilience, and influence among young people. Funding and Benefits Filmmakers selected from the database will receive: €765 to €1,800 per project activity (per filmmaker) Opportunities to collaborate with international organizations Increased visibility across Africa and Europe Portfolio development through impactful storytelling Access to cross-continental networks Payment structure: 50% at the start of the assignment 50% upon successful completion and approval What You’ll Be Doing As part of this initiative, filmmakers will: Produce documentaries and short-form social media content Capture real-life stories of youth impact and advocacy Film events, interviews, and community activities Deliver high-quality edits, subtitles, and transcripts Collaborate with program partners and incorporate feedback Who Should Apply This opportunity is ideal for young creatives who: Are between 18 and 35 years old Have experience in documentary filmmaking or storytelling Are skilled in filming, editing, and sound production Own or have access to filming equipment Are passionate about youth engagement and social impact Are willing to travel when required Eligible Countries Applicants must be based in one of the following countries: Burkina Faso, Burundi, Belgium, Cameroon, Ghana, Kenya, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone, South Sudan, Tanzania, Tunisia, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe. How to Apply Interested applicants must submit their application through the Young Filmmakers Database Application form (Google Form available on the official platform). Applications submitted via email will not be accepted If applying as a team, only one member should complete the form Be sure to review the Terms of Reference before applying (available in English and French). APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more grant opportunities, click here . Deadline This is a rolling application , meaning submissions are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, positions are limited and applications may close once the database is filled. Early application is strongly encouraged. Why This Opportunity Matters This initiative is more than just a filmmaking gig—it’s a platform to: Amplify youth voices Influence policy and decision-making Tell authentic stories of resilience and change Be part of a global movement for youth empowerment If you have the skills and passion to tell stories that matter, don’t miss this opportunity. title: CODESRIA/ZASB Summer School 2026 in African Studies: Fully Funded Opportunity for PhD Students and Early Career Scholars in Africa
summary: The 7th CODESRIA/ZASB Summer School in African Studies and Area Studies in Africa 2026 is now open for applications, offering a prestigious and intellectually transformative opportunity for PhD students and early career scholars across Africa. Scheduled to take place from 20–24 July 2026 in Dakar, Senegal , this Summer School is organized by the Council for the Development of Social Science Research in Africa (CODESRIA) in collaboration with the Centre for African Studies in Basel (ZASB) . The programme is supported by the Oumou Dilly Foundation and aims to strengthen interdisciplinary scholarship rooted in African perspectives. This year’s theme, “Honouring Mudimbe: Rethinking Research through African Thought,” invites participants to critically engage with African epistemologies and reimagine the frameworks through which knowledge about Africa is produced. About the Summer School The CODESRIA/ZASB Summer School is a high-level academic programme designed to foster critical thinking, interdisciplinary engagement, and intellectual innovation among emerging scholars in the social sciences and humanities. The programme emphasizes the importance of African-centered knowledge production , encouraging participants to move beyond traditional frameworks and engage deeply with African intellectual traditions, philosophies, and methodologies. Participants will explore how African Studies can serve not only as a field of inquiry but also as a source of theoretical and conceptual innovation with global relevance. Theme: Honouring Mudimbe – Rethinking Research through African Thought The 2026 Summer School pays tribute to the late Valentin-Yves Mudimbe , one of Africa’s most influential thinkers. His work challenged colonial knowledge systems and introduced new ways of understanding Africa through concepts such as: The “Colonial Library” The “Invention of Africa” The programme invites participants to reflect on and respond to Mudimbe’s intellectual legacy by addressing key questions such as: How do existing academic frameworks shape our understanding of Africa? How can African epistemologies reshape global knowledge systems? What does it mean to produce knowledge from an African perspective? How can African Studies contribute to global theoretical innovation? This approach encourages scholars to move from critique to conceptual reconstruction , positioning African thought at the center of global academic discourse. Objectives of the Summer School The programme is designed to achieve the following goals: Provide a platform for PhD students and early career scholars to engage with advanced theoretical and methodological debates Promote interdisciplinary and transdisciplinary research approaches Strengthen African-centered scholarship and intellectual identity Foster a community of scholars committed to critical inquiry and innovation Encourage participants to link academic research with societal impact What the Programme Offers Participants selected for the Summer School will benefit from: 1. Academic and Intellectual Development Engagement with leading African and international scholars Exposure to cutting-edge debates in African Studies Opportunities to refine research concepts and methodologies 2. Networking Opportunities Interaction with peers and experts from across Africa and beyond Inclusion in a vibrant scholarly community Opportunities for long-term academic collaboration 3. Fully Funded Support (for African Participants) Travel costs Accommodation Meals during the programme A limited number of self-funded participants may also be accepted. Who Can Apply The programme is open to: PhD students in Social Sciences and Humanities Early career researchers affiliated with higher education institutions Applicants based in African institutions (priority given) PhD students from Swiss universities Preference is given to candidates in the first or second year of their PhD programme . Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package including: Motivation letter (maximum 500 words) Concept paper (maximum 2,500 words), including: Research focus Relevance to African epistemologies and Mudimbe’s work Expectations from the Summer School Suggested reading for discussion Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV) One letter of recommendation Passport copy Proof of PhD registration or doctoral certificate Incomplete applications will not be considered. Important Guidelines All submissions will undergo plagiarism and AI-content detection checks Applications must demonstrate originality and academic integrity Candidates are encouraged to present innovative and critical perspectives Application Timeline Application Deadline: May 1, 2026 Programme Dates: July 20–24, 2026 Applicants are advised to prepare their documents early and submit complete applications before the deadline. How to Apply Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official portal: Apply for the CODESRIA Summer School 2026 For inquiries, contact: Email: tgf@codesria.org Why You Should Apply The CODESRIA/ZASB Summer School offers a unique opportunity to: Deepen your understanding of African-centered research methodologies Engage with leading thinkers in African Studies Strengthen your academic and research skills Build a strong scholarly network Contribute to shaping the future of global knowledge production Click HERE to learn more about the 7th CODESRIA/ZASB Summer School in African Studies and Area Studies in Africa 2026. Discover more research opportunities on OFY HERE and more fellowship opportunities HERE . The post CODESRIA/ZASB Summer School 2026 in African Studies: Fully Funded Opportunity for PhD Students and Early Career Scholars in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
